preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an intent-aware, ai-first design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is an intent-aware, AI-first design system built for startups. At the decision stage, Validators need to quickly confirm they're in the right place before evaluating proof, so a clear, confident hero anchors the page.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require trust signals early. Showing customer logos, testimonials, or authority markers from recognizable startups reassures this audience that MODE is production-proven and de-risks their decision before they dig into evidence.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: The Validator archetype demands hard proof. Presenting concrete metrics — adoption rates, performance benchmarks, time-to-ship improvements, or case study data — satisfies their need to justify the purchase decision with quantifiable outcomes.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: At the decision funnel stage, plan comparison and pricing transparency are essential. Framing tiers clearly lets Validators self-select and mentally commit, removing the last friction point between consideration and action.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. A strong, singular CTA ('Start your paid plan') capitalizes on the proof-then-pricing narrative arc, converting the Validator's now-justified confidence into a commitment action.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat provides secondary paths — contact sales, explore docs, compare plans in detail — ensuring Validators who need one more data point or a different entry path aren't lost entirely.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context: MODE is an intent-aware, AI-first design system built for startups. At the decision stage, Validators need to quickly confirm they're in the right place before evaluating proof, so a clear, confident hero anchors the page.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require trust signals early. Showing customer logos, testimonials, or authority markers from recognizable startups reassures this audience that MODE is production-proven and de-risks their decision before they dig into evidence.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: The Validator archetype demands hard proof. Presenting concrete metrics — adoption rates, performance benchmarks, time-to-ship improvements, or case study data — satisfies their need to justify the purchase decision with quantifiable outcomes.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: At the decision funnel stage, plan comparison and pricing transparency are essential. Framing tiers clearly lets Validators self-select and mentally commit, removing the last friction point between consideration and action.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. A strong, singular CTA ('Start your paid plan') capitalizes on the proof-then-pricing narrative arc, converting the Validator's now-justified confidence into a commitment action.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: At decision stage, conversion focus requires minimal variant to 'strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' — the persistent CTA is the most converted element on SaaS pages.
Conversion funnel stage requires text-only variant that 'orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below' — slim title and subhead, no media or secondary CTA.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer for code-first SaaS teams. Components that resolve to the right behavior, copy register, and visual emphasis — for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust signals; with-photo variant 'increases engagement and perceived authenticity' and should be placed 'near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.'

slots.quote: We were building four variants of every landing section by hand — hero for paid, hero for organic, hero for trial. MODE collapsed that into a single brief. We shipped the full funnel in a day instead of a sprint.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands credibility; 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.'

stats.label: Median time from install to first working brief output
Variants produced per brief — Mover, Validator, and Explorer in parallel
Components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent

page.reasoning: Conversion stage requires highlighted variant for recommended plan, and 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list' to support the decision moment.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer — resolution engine, IA planner, multi-variant generation, palette map, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever, in your codebase, no subscription.
features: Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Bring your own Anthropic API key — you own the spend

page.reasoning: Validator variant should 'include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required')' — centered positioning works for terminal conversion actions at decision stage.

slots.headline: The decision is already made. Now make it official.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Full ownership. Running in under an hour — no subscription, no lock-in, no Figma handoff cycles.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No recurring fees. Extend and fork freely.

page.reasoning: Conversion page requires with-cta variant to 'recapture departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
